subject: cs
prompt: Generate 5 diagram-based questions on Quantum Error Correction (QEC). Cover: stabilizer codes and syndrome measurement circuits, the 3-qubit bit-flip code with error detection circuit, the surface code lattice and its logical qubits, fault-tolerant gate teleportation, and the threshold theorem with error rate diagrams. Each question must require the student to analyze or interpret a diagram.

questions.question: Analyze the stabilizer code in the diagram below and explain how the code detects and corrects a single bit-flip error. Identify the role of each stabilizer and how they contribute to error detection.

diagram.description:
Diagram of a simple stabilizer code illustrating error detection using syndromes. The diagram should show three data qubits and two stabilizer generators. Each stabilizer generator is connected to two of the data qubits, forming a check that can detect a single bit-flip error. Label the qubits as q1, q2, q3 and the stabilizers as S1, S2. Indicate the connections between stabilizers and qubits with lines, and show how a bit-flip error on one qubit affects the stabilizers' syndromes.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • Hamiltonian
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear.

questions.correctAnswer: In the diagram below, stabilizer codes use a set of operators (stabilizers) that commute with the error-free system's Hamiltonian. Each stabilizer, as depicted in the diagram, is a combination of Pauli matrices. When a bit-flip error occurs, the resulting state becomes an eigenstate with an eigenvalue of -1 for one or more stabilizers shown in the diagram. By measuring these stabilizers, syndromes (patterns of measurement results) are obtained, revealing the locations of errors. The corrections are then applied to the error-affected qubits based on the syndromes, as illustrated in the circuit shown.
questions.rubric: 1. Identify each stabilizer's role in the diagram (3 points). 2. Explain the measurement of syndromes using the diagram (3 points). 3. Describe error correction based on syndromes as shown in the diagram (4 points).
questions.text: Analyze the stabilizer code in the diagram below and explain how the code detects and corrects a single bit-flip error. Identify the role of each stabilizer and how they contribute to error detection.

questions.question: Given the circuit shown below of the 3-qubit bit-flip code, describe the process of detecting a single bit-flip error, including the logic involved in the syndrome measurement circuit.

diagram.description:
3-qubit bit-flip code circuit with syndrome measurement subcircuit. The circuit includes three qubit lines labeled q0, q1, and q2. The encoding section uses CNOT gates to entangle the qubits. The syndrome measurement subcircuit includes additional CNOT gates and measurement symbols to detect a single bit-flip error. The circuit should clearly show the flow from encoding to error detection, with labeled qubit lines and gates.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • CNOT
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear.

questions.correctAnswer: The 3-qubit bit-flip code, as illustrated in the diagram below, encodes one logical qubit into three physical qubits. If one qubit undergoes a bit-flip error, the syndrome measurement circuit, shown in the diagram, uses ancilla qubits to measure parity checks between the qubits. The syndromes, as combinations of parities, indicate which qubit has flipped. Logic gates such as CNOT, depicted in the circuit, are used for parity checks, and based on the syndrome output, a correction is applied on the erroneous qubit.
questions.rubric:
1. Describe the function of the bit-flip encoding and decoding steps as shown in the diagram (3 points). 
2. Explain the syndrome measurement process using the circuit shown (4 points). 
3. Detail correction logic based on syndromes as depicted in the diagram (3 points).
questions.text: Given the circuit shown below of the 3-qubit bit-flip code, describe the process of detecting a single bit-flip error, including the logic involved in the syndrome measurement circuit.

questions.question: Examine the surface code lattice in the diagram below, and explain how logical qubits are formed and manipulated within the code.

diagram.description:
Surface code lattice diagram showing a grid of physical qubits. Each node represents a physical qubit, arranged in a 2D lattice. Highlight the logical qubits by marking specific paths or clusters of physical qubits. Include labels for data qubits and ancilla qubits, and indicate logical operations such as X and Z stabilizers. Use a clear and organized layout to represent the surface code structure, emphasizing the connections and interactions between qubits.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • logical operator
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear.

questions.correctAnswer: In the surface code, logical qubits are formed by defining extended regions of collective states on a 2D lattice of physical qubits as shown in the diagram below. The code uses a mix of Z and X stabilizer plaquettes, depicted in the diagram, to detect errors. Logical operators are generally long chains of physical operators across the lattice, which can be visualized in the diagram. Logical operations involve moving or braiding these logical qubits by adjusting plaquette boundaries or links, as illustrated in the diagram, which requires careful synthesis and error correction at each step.
questions.rubric: 1. Explain the formation of logical qubits using stabilizer patterns as shown in the diagram (4 points). 2. Describe manipulation techniques on the lattices, referencing the diagram below (3 points). 3. Discuss the role of error correction during operations, with reference to the diagram (3 points).
questions.text: Examine the surface code lattice in the diagram below, and explain how logical qubits are formed and manipulated within the code.

questions.question: Interpret the fault-tolerant gate teleportation in the diagram below, and describe the procedure for teleporting a quantum gate in a fault-tolerant quantum computing system.

diagram.description: CUMULATIVE LEAK CORRECTIONS (all must be fixed): Known answer terms to avoid: Initially; The diagram labels the distinct stages of the procedure ("Entanglement", "Gate Application", "Measurement", "Correction"), which directly answers the question asking the student to describe the procedure.. Remove the labels "Entanglement", "Gate Application", "Measurement", and "Correction". Keep all other labels and the overall circuit structure. The diagram should show the quantum circuit for gate teleportation, including qubits q0, q1, and ancilla, H and CNOT gates, measurement gates, and classical feedback for correction, but without explicitly labeling the procedural steps.

questions.correctAnswer: Gate teleportation in a fault-tolerant system, as illustrated in the diagram below, involves using an entangled pair of qubits and sending a gate operation through a combination of teleportation steps. Initially, an encoded magic state is prepared, as depicted in the circuit shown. Then, through a series of measurements and corrections indicated in the diagram, auxiliary qubits allow the operation of the desired gate on a different qubit as if it was teleported there. This process, shown in the diagram, bypasses direct physical application of the gate and maintains fault tolerance.
questions.rubric: 1. Describe the preparation of the entangled state for teleportation as shown in the diagram (3 points). 2. Explain the steps involved in teleporting the gate operation, referencing the sequence in the diagram (4 points). 3. Discuss how fault tolerance is preserved during the process, as indicated in the circuit shown (3 points).
questions.text: Interpret the fault-tolerant gate teleportation in the diagram below, and describe the procedure for teleporting a quantum gate in a fault-tolerant quantum computing system.

questions.question: Analyze the threshold theorem in the diagram below, and explain how it relates the error rates of physical qubits to logical qubit error correction thresholds.

diagram.description:
CUMULATIVE LEAK CORRECTIONS (all must be fixed): Known answer terms to avoid: threshold; The diagram explicitly labels the regions as "Error correction effective" and "Error correction ineffective", which is the core conclusion the student is supposed to derive from analyzing the graph's shape.. A diagram illustrating the threshold theorem in quantum computing. The diagram should show a graph with error rates on the x-axis and logical qubit reliability on the y-axis. Include a curve that represents the threshold theorem, indicating a critical threshold point where error correction becomes effective. Label the axes as 'Physical Qubit Error Rate' and 'Logical Qubit Error Correction'. Highlight the threshold point where the error rate drops below the threshold, improving logical qubit reliability. Add annotations to explain the relationship between physical and logical qubit error rates in the context of the threshold theorem.

FORBIDDEN LABELS (do NOT render as text, legend entries, annotations, note boxes, or titles — these reveal the answer):
  • threshold
The diagram will FAIL review if any of these appear. IMPORTANT correction needed: The diagram explicitly labels the regions as "Error correction effective" and "Error correction ineffective", which is the core conclusion the student is supposed to derive from analyzing the graph's shape.. Specifically: The labels "Error correction effective", "Error correction ineffective", "Logical error decreases", and ". Do NOT include region labels (Feasible/Infeasible, Stable/Unstable, Optimal, etc.), formula annotations, reaction product labels, threshold markers, or any text that reveals the answer. Show structure and context only.

questions.correctAnswer: The threshold theorem states that quantum computation can be performed reliably provided the physical qubit error rate is below a certain threshold. The diagram below includes curves showing how logical error rates decrease exponentially with increasing code size when the physical error rate is below the threshold. If the physical error rate is above the threshold, logical error rates don't improve adequately. The diagram illustrates encoding, error correction, syndrome measurement, and repair mechanisms working together to maintain reliability.
questions.rubric:
1. Explain the concept of the threshold theorem (3 points).
2. Relate physical error rates to threshold limits illustrated in the diagram below (4 points).
3. Describe the role of error correction in maintaining logical qubit reliability as shown in the diagram (3 points).
questions.text: Analyze the threshold theorem in the diagram below, and explain how it relates the error rates of physical qubits to logical qubit error correction thresholds.
